marapendi
  • Components
  • View page source

Components

Physical building blocks of an electrochemical cell — electrodes, membrane, flow channels, and supporting structures.

Component classes are dataclasses that hold geometric and material parameters only — no physics equations. Computation is delegated to the strategy model objects owned by CellBaseModel (see Models).

class marapendi.components.cell.Cell(name: str = 'cell', area: float = 1.0, electrical_resistance: float = 0.0, thermal_resistance: float = 0.0, ca: ~marapendi.components.cell.CellSide = <factory>, an: ~marapendi.components.cell.CellSide = <factory>, memb: ~marapendi.components.membrane.Membrane = <factory>, charge: str = 'proton')[source]

Bases: Updatable

Geometry, material parameters, and layer stack for an AEM/PEM cell.

Physics models (membrane transport, gas diffusion, liquid transport, catalyst-layer kinetics, voltage) are intentionally excluded from this class. They belong on the BaseModel that wraps the simulation, keeping component data separate from the equations that act on it.

an: CellSide
area: float = 1.0
build_property_arrays()[source]
ca: CellSide
charge: str = 'proton'
electrical_resistance: float = 0.0
get_property_array(property_name: str)[source]

Get an array of a scalar or vector property across all layers.

  • Scalar property → shape (n_layers, 1)

  • Length-N array → shape (n_layers, N)

Layers missing the attribute contribute np.nan.

memb: Membrane
name: str = 'cell'
thermal_resistance: float = 0.0
class marapendi.components.cell.CellSide(name: str = 'side', cl: ~marapendi.components.porous_layers.PorousLayer = <factory>, gdl: ~marapendi.components.porous_layers.PorousLayer = <factory>, mpl: ~marapendi.components.porous_layers.PorousLayer = <factory>, ch: ~marapendi.components.flow_channels.FlowChannel = <factory>, has_mpl: bool = False, has_gdl: bool = True)[source]

Bases: Updatable

One side (anode or cathode) of an AEM/PEM cell.

ch: FlowChannel
cl: PorousLayer
gdl: PorousLayer
has_gdl: bool = True
has_mpl: bool = False
mpl: PorousLayer
name: str = 'side'

CellState — snapshot of all derived thermodynamic quantities at a given instant, produced by TransientCellModel._compute_derived_quantities.

Storing both full per-layer arrays and pre-computed layer slices means every downstream method (_compute_resistances, _compute_fluxes, VoltageModel methods, …) can receive a single state object instead of an unpacked tuple of 10+ arrays.

class marapendi.components.cell_state.CellState(x: ndarray, lmbd: ndarray, T: ndarray, cg_k: ndarray, s: ndarray, iF: object, p_g: ndarray, p_g_k: ndarray, D_g_k: ndarray, c_sat: ndarray, c_v: ndarray, rh: ndarray, rho_l: ndarray, nu_l: ndarray, M_k: ndarray, f_v: ndarray, T_memb: ndarray, T_ca_cl: ndarray, T_an_cl: ndarray, f_v_memb: ndarray, f_v_ca_cl: ndarray, lmbd_ca_cl: ndarray, p_h2: ndarray, p_o2_ca_cl: ndarray)[source]

Bases: object

Thermodynamic state derived from the ODE state vector x.

Full arrays have shape (n_layers, ...). Sliced fields (suffixed with a layer label) have shape (...,) and are numpy views into the corresponding full array.

Parameters:

  • lmbd (ndarray) – Ionomer water content [–].

  • T (ndarray) – Temperature [K].

  • cg_k (ndarray, shape (n_layers, n_species, ...)) – Gas-phase molar concentrations [kmol m⁻³].

  • s (ndarray) – Liquid water saturation [–].

  • iF (ndarray or float) – Faradaic current density normalised by Faraday constant [kmol m⁻² s⁻¹].

  • p_g (ndarray) – Total gas pressure [Pa].

  • p_g_k (ndarray, shape (n_layers, n_species, ...)) – Species partial pressures [Pa].

  • D_g_k (ndarray, shape (n_layers, n_species, ...)) – Gas-phase diffusion coefficients per species.

  • c_sat (ndarray) – Water vapour saturation concentration [kmol m⁻³].

  • c_v (ndarray) – Water vapour concentration [kmol m⁻³].

  • rh (ndarray) – Relative humidity [–].

  • rho_l (ndarray) – Liquid water density [kg m⁻³].

  • nu_l (ndarray) – Liquid water kinematic viscosity [m² s⁻¹].

  • M_k (ndarray, shape (n_layers, n_species, ...)) – Species molar-mass fractions [kg kmol⁻¹].

  • f_v (ndarray) – Ionomer water volume fraction [–].

  • fields (Pre-sliced convenience)

  • ------------------------------

  • T_memb (ndarray)

  • T_ca_cl (ndarray)

  • T_an_cl (ndarray)

  • f_v_memb (ndarray)

  • f_v_ca_cl (ndarray)

  • lmbd_ca_cl (ndarray)

  • p_h2 (ndarray — H2 partial pressure at anode CL)

  • p_o2_ca_cl (ndarray — O2 partial pressure at cathode CL)

D_g_k: ndarray
M_k: ndarray
T: ndarray
T_an_cl: ndarray
T_ca_cl: ndarray
T_memb: ndarray
c_sat: ndarray
c_v: ndarray
cg_k: ndarray
f_v: ndarray
f_v_ca_cl: ndarray
f_v_memb: ndarray
iF: object
lmbd: ndarray
lmbd_ca_cl: ndarray
nu_l: ndarray
p_g: ndarray
p_g_k: ndarray
p_h2: ndarray
p_o2_ca_cl: ndarray
rh: ndarray
rho_l: ndarray
s: ndarray
x: ndarray

Catalyst-layer component dataclasses.

These dataclasses hold geometric, structural, and material parameters for catalyst layers. Electrokinetic and transport computations (ionomer film O₂ resistance, charge resistance, water-film thickness, etc.) are implemented as stateless strategy methods in marapendi.models.catalyst_layer (CatalystLayerModel, PtCCatalystLayerModel).

class marapendi.components.catalyst_layers.CatalystLayer(rho_dry_ion: float = None, EW_ion: float = None, darken_num_ion: ~numpy.ndarray = None, darken_den_ion: ~numpy.ndarray = None, sorption_coeffs_ion: ~numpy.ndarray = None, lmbd_liq_ref_ion: float = None, D_lmbd_ref_ion: float = None, k_des_ref_ion: float = None, sigma_ref_ion: float = 1e-12, T_ref_sigma_ion: float = None, T_ref_D_ion: float = None, T_ref_des_ion: float = None, E_act_ion: float = None, f_v_perc_ion: float = None, n_sigma_ion: float = None, c_ion: float = None, V_ion: float = None, charge_ion: str = 'proton', name: str = 'porous layer', thickness: float = 0.0001, bulk_density: float = 2000.0, bulk_electrical_conductivity: float = 10000.0, bulk_specific_heat_capacity: float = 1000.0, bulk_thermal_conductivity: float = 1.0, eps_p: float = 0.6, tort: float = 1.0, d_p: float = 2e-05, K_abs: float = 1e-11, n_rel: float = 3, n_s: float = 3, theta_contact: float = 95.0, eps_ion: float = 0.3, tau_ion: float = 1.0, p_b: float = 15020, van_genuchten_m: float = 0.7262, van_genuchten_n: float = 3.652, s_im: float = 0, ionomer: ~marapendi.components.ionomer.Ionomer = None, reaction: ~marapendi.models.electrochemistry.ElectrochemicalReaction = <factory>, catalyst_loading: float = 0.002, ionomer_to_catalyst_ratio: float = 0.75, catalyst_density: float = 21450, ecsa: float = 70000.0, t_ion_film: float = 2e-09, theta_catalyst: float = 0, electrolyte_saturation: float = 0)[source]

Bases: PorousLayer, Ionomer

Base dataclass for a generic catalyst layer.

Combines porous-layer geometry (PorousLayer) with ionomer material parameters (Ionomer).

Electrokinetic and transport computations (charge resistance, O₂ film resistance, water-film thickness) are implemented in marapendi.models.catalyst_layer.

ionomer

Ionomer parameter object whose fields are copied onto self during __post_init__ for direct attribute access.

Type:

Ionomer, optional

reaction

Electrochemical reaction parameters.

Type:

ElectrochemicalReaction

catalyst_loading

Catalyst loading [kg/m²] (default: 0.2 mg/cm²).

Type:

float

ionomer_to_catalyst_ratio

Ionomer-to-catalyst mass ratio.

Type:

float

catalyst_density

Density of catalyst particles [kg/m³].

Type:

float

ecsa

Electrochemically active surface area [m²/kg].

Type:

float

eps_ion

Volume fraction of ionomer in the layer [-].

Type:

float

t_ion_film

Ionomer film thickness around catalyst particles [m].

Type:

float

theta_contact

Contact angle [degrees].

Type:

float

theta_catalyst

Catalyst surface coverage [-].

Type:

float

electrolyte_saturation

Fraction of pore volume filled by liquid electrolyte [-].

Type:

float

__eq__(other)[source]

Use identity comparison — layers are unique instances in the cell model. This avoids ambiguous truth-value errors from numpy array fields during membership tests like layer in self.ionomer_layers.

__post_init__()[source]

Copy all ionomer fields onto self so ionomer properties are directly accessible (e.g. self.dry_density, self.equivalent_weight). Skipped if no ionomer was supplied.

catalyst_density: float = 21450
catalyst_loading: float = 0.002
ecsa: float = 70000.0
electrolyte_saturation: float = 0
eps_ion: float = 0.3
ionomer: Ionomer = None
ionomer_to_catalyst_ratio: float = 0.75
reaction: ElectrochemicalReaction
t_ion_film: float = 2e-09
theta_catalyst: float = 0
theta_contact: float = 95.0
class marapendi.components.catalyst_layers.PtCCatalystLayer(rho_dry_ion: float = None, EW_ion: float = None, darken_num_ion: ~numpy.ndarray = None, darken_den_ion: ~numpy.ndarray = None, sorption_coeffs_ion: ~numpy.ndarray = None, lmbd_liq_ref_ion: float = None, D_lmbd_ref_ion: float = None, k_des_ref_ion: float = None, sigma_ref_ion: float = 1e-12, T_ref_sigma_ion: float = None, T_ref_D_ion: float = None, T_ref_des_ion: float = None, E_act_ion: float = None, f_v_perc_ion: float = None, n_sigma_ion: float = None, c_ion: float = None, V_ion: float = None, charge_ion: str = 'proton', name: str = 'porous layer', thickness: float = 1e-05, bulk_density: float = 2000.0, bulk_electrical_conductivity: float = 10000.0, bulk_specific_heat_capacity: float = 1000.0, bulk_thermal_conductivity: float = 1.0, eps_p: float = 0, tort: float = 1.0, d_p: float = 2e-05, K_abs: float = 1e-11, n_rel: float = 3, n_s: float = 3, theta_contact: float = 95.0, eps_ion: float = 0, tau_ion: float = 1.0, p_b: float = 15020, van_genuchten_m: float = 0.7262, van_genuchten_n: float = 3.652, s_im: float = 0, ionomer: ~marapendi.components.ionomer.Ionomer = None, reaction: ~marapendi.models.electrochemistry.ElectrochemicalReaction = <factory>, catalyst_loading: float = 0.002, ionomer_to_catalyst_ratio: float = 0.75, catalyst_density: float = 21450, ecsa: float = 70000.0, t_ion_film: float = 0, theta_catalyst: float = 0, electrolyte_saturation: float = 0, L_Pt: float = 0.002, wt_Pt: float = 0.5, ic_ratio: float = 0.75, rho_Pt: float = 21450, rho_C: float = 1950, a_Pt: float = 0, r_C: float = 2.5e-08, dry_eps_ion: float = 0, omega_PtO: float = 3000000.0)[source]

Bases: CatalystLayer

Catalyst layer model with explicit platinum/carbon and ionomer structure.

L_Pt

Pt loading [g/cm²].

Type:

float

wt_Pt

Pt weight percent in catalyst.

Type:

float

ic_ratio

Ionomer/carbon mass ratio.

Type:

float

rho_Pt, rho_C

Densities [kg/m³].

Type:

float

r_C

Radius of carbon agglomerates [m].

Type:

float

L_Pt: float = 0.002
__post_init__()[source]

Compute volume fractions and geometry based on catalyst composition.

Equations come from Hao et al. (2015).

References

Hao, L. et al. J. Electrochem. Soc. 162, F854 (2015).

a_Pt: float = 0
dry_eps_ion: float = 0
eps_ion: float = 0
eps_p: float = 0
ic_ratio: float = 0.75
omega_PtO: float = 3000000.0
r_C: float = 2.5e-08
rho_C: float = 1950
rho_Pt: float = 21450
t_ion_film: float = 0
theta_contact: float = 95.0
thickness: float = 1e-05
update_ionomer_film_volume(ionomer_expansion_factor=1)[source]

Update eps_ion, eps_p, t_ion_film and derived geometry for given water content. For now marapendi do not consider variations of the ionomer volume. This function is only called at __post_init__.

wt_Pt: float = 0.5

Module providing classes to model porous layers in electrochemical cells.

class marapendi.components.porous_layers.PorousLayer(name: str = 'porous layer', thickness: float = 0.0001, bulk_density: float = 2000.0, bulk_electrical_conductivity: float = 10000.0, bulk_specific_heat_capacity: float = 1000.0, bulk_thermal_conductivity: float = 1.0, eps_p: float = 0.6, tort: float = 1.0, d_p: float = 2e-05, K_abs: float = 1e-11, n_rel: float = 3, n_s: float = 3, theta_contact: float = 120.0, eps_ion: float = 0.0, tau_ion: float = 1.0, p_b: float = 15020, van_genuchten_m: float = 0.7262, van_genuchten_n: float = 3.652, s_im: float = 0)[source]

Bases: Layer

Represents a porous layer in a fuel cell or electrolyzer, defining its properties related to gas transport, permeability, capillarity, and thermal behavior.

thickness

Thickness of the porous layer in meters (default is 1e-3 m).

Type:

float

gas

Gas composition object representing the gas properties in the layer.

Type:

GasComposition

eps_p

Porosity of the layer (0 < eps_p < 1).

Type:

float

effective_gas_diffusion_ratio

Ratio accounting for effective gas diffusion through the porous medium (default is 1).

Type:

float

d_p

Average pore diameter in meters (default is large, so Knudsen diffusion negligible).

Type:

float

transport_resistance_model

Model used to calculate gas transport resistance.

Type:

PorousGasResistanceModel

two_phase_transport_model

Model used to compute liquid water flow and capillarity.

Type:

DarcyTransportModel

non_wetting_saturation

Fraction of the pore volume occupied by non-wetting phase (0 to 1).

Type:

float

thermal_conductivity

Thermal conductivity in W/(m·K).

Type:

float

K_abs

Absolute permeability in m².

Type:

float

n_rel

Exponent for relative permeability model (default is 3, often used for cubic relationship).

Type:

float

theta_contact

Contact angle for liquid water in degrees (default is 120°).

Type:

float

non_wetting_phase

Non-wetting phase (liquid or gas).

Type:

str

Notes

This class internally computes quantities like capillary pressure scaling and liquid flow resistance based on current temperature, saturation, and geometry.

K_abs: float = 1e-11
calculate_bulk_thermal_resistance()[source]

Computes the thermal resistance of the layer.

Returns:

Thermal resistance in m²·K/W.

Return type:

float

d_p: float = 2e-05
eps_ion: float = 0.0
eps_p: float = 0.6
n_rel: float = 3
n_s: float = 3
name: str = 'porous layer'
p_b: float = 15020
s_im: float = 0
set_water_film_thickness(water_saturation)[source]
tau_ion: float = 1.0
theta_contact: float = 120.0
tort: float = 1.0
update_ionomer_film_volume(ionomer_water_content, temperature)[source]
van_genuchten_m: float = 0.7262
van_genuchten_n: float = 3.652

Membrane component dataclasses.

These dataclasses store geometric and material parameters for proton- or anion-exchange membranes. Physics computations (water volume fraction, diffusion coefficients, sorption isotherms, permeation flux, ionic conductivity, etc.) are implemented as stateless strategy methods in marapendi.models.membrane (IonomerModel, MembraneModel, PFSAModel).

class marapendi.components.membrane.Membrane(rho_dry_ion: float = None, EW_ion: float = None, darken_num_ion: ndarray = None, darken_den_ion: ndarray = None, sorption_coeffs_ion: ndarray = None, lmbd_liq_ref_ion: float = None, D_lmbd_ref_ion: float = None, k_des_ref_ion: float = None, sigma_ref_ion: float = 1e-12, T_ref_sigma_ion: float = None, T_ref_D_ion: float = None, T_ref_des_ion: float = None, E_act_ion: float = None, f_v_perc_ion: float = None, n_sigma_ion: float = None, c_ion: float = None, V_ion: float = None, charge_ion: str = 'proton', name: str = 'porous layer', thickness: float = 0.0001, bulk_density: float = 2000.0, bulk_electrical_conductivity: float = 10000.0, bulk_specific_heat_capacity: float = 1000.0, bulk_thermal_conductivity: float = 1.0, eps_p: float = 0.6, tort: float = 1.0, d_p: float = 2e-05, K_abs: float = 1e-11, n_rel: float = 3, n_s: float = 3, theta_contact: float = 120.0, eps_ion: float = 1.0, tau_ion: float = 1.0, p_b: float = 15020, van_genuchten_m: float = 0.7262, van_genuchten_n: float = 3.652, s_im: float = 0, tort_ion: float = 1.0, ionomer: Ionomer = None, thermal_conductivity: float = 0.9, specific_heat_capacity: float = 2000.0)[source]

Bases: PorousLayer, Ionomer

Base dataclass for proton- or anion-exchange membranes.

Combines porous-layer geometry (PorousLayer) with ionomer material parameters (Ionomer).

All physics (water volume fraction, diffusion coefficients, sorption isotherms, H₂ permeation flux, ionic conductivity, electroosmotic drag) are computed by the corresponding model classes in marapendi.models.membrane.

thickness

Membrane thickness [m].

Type:

float

eps_ion

Volume fraction of ionomer [-] (default 1 — pure ionomer).

Type:

float

tort_ion

Ionomer tortuosity factor [-] (default 1).

Type:

float

ionomer

Ionomer parameter object whose fields are copied onto self during __post_init__ for direct attribute access.

Type:

Ionomer, optional

thermal_conductivity

Thermal conductivity [W/(m·K)] (default 0.9).

Type:

float

specific_heat_capacity

Specific heat capacity [J/(kg·K)] (default 2000).

Type:

float

__eq__(other)[source]

Use identity comparison — layers are unique instances in the cell model. This avoids ambiguous truth-value errors from numpy array fields during membership tests like layer in self.ionomer_layers.

__post_init__()[source]

Copy all ionomer fields onto self so ionomer properties are directly accessible (e.g. self.dry_density, self.equivalent_weight). Skipped if no ionomer was supplied.

eps_ion: float = 1.0
ionomer: Ionomer = None
specific_heat_capacity: float = 2000.0
thermal_conductivity: float = 0.9
tort_ion: float = 1.0
update_ionomer_film_volume(ionomer_expansion_factor=1)[source]

Update eps_ion, eps_p, t_ion_film and derived geometry for given water content.

class marapendi.components.membrane.PFSAMembrane(rho_dry_ion: float = None, EW_ion: float = None, darken_num_ion: numpy.ndarray = None, darken_den_ion: numpy.ndarray = None, sorption_coeffs_ion: numpy.ndarray = None, lmbd_liq_ref_ion: float = None, D_lmbd_ref_ion: float = None, k_des_ref_ion: float = None, sigma_ref_ion: float = 1e-12, T_ref_sigma_ion: float = None, T_ref_D_ion: float = None, T_ref_des_ion: float = None, E_act_ion: float = None, f_v_perc_ion: float = None, n_sigma_ion: float = None, c_ion: float = None, V_ion: float = None, charge_ion: str = 'proton', name: str = 'porous layer', thickness: float = 0.0001, bulk_density: float = 2000.0, bulk_electrical_conductivity: float = 10000.0, bulk_specific_heat_capacity: float = 1000.0, bulk_thermal_conductivity: float = 1.0, eps_p: float = 0.6, tort: float = 1.0, d_p: float = 2e-05, K_abs: float = 1e-11, n_rel: float = 3, n_s: float = 3, theta_contact: float = 120.0, eps_ion: float = 1.0, tau_ion: float = 1.0, p_b: float = 15020, van_genuchten_m: float = 0.7262, van_genuchten_n: float = 3.652, s_im: float = 0, tort_ion: float = 1.0, ionomer: marapendi.components.ionomer.Ionomer = None, thermal_conductivity: float = 0.9, specific_heat_capacity: float = 2000.0)[source]

Bases: Membrane, PFSAIonomer

Ionomer component dataclasses.

Classes

Ionomer : Base dataclass holding ionomer material parameters. PFSAIonomer : Parameter dataclass for perfluorosulfonic acid ionomers (e.g. Nafion).

These classes carry physical and electrochemical parameters only. All computation (conductivity, permeability, water uptake, drag coefficients, etc.) is implemented in the corresponding model classes in marapendi.models.membrane.

class marapendi.components.ionomer.Ionomer(rho_dry_ion: float = None, EW_ion: float = None, darken_num_ion: ndarray = None, darken_den_ion: ndarray = None, sorption_coeffs_ion: ndarray = None, lmbd_liq_ref_ion: float = None, D_lmbd_ref_ion: float = None, k_des_ref_ion: float = None, sigma_ref_ion: float = 1e-12, T_ref_sigma_ion: float = None, T_ref_D_ion: float = None, T_ref_des_ion: float = None, E_act_ion: float = None, f_v_perc_ion: float = None, n_sigma_ion: float = None, c_ion: float = None, V_ion: float = None, charge_ion: str = 'proton')[source]

Bases: Updatable

Base dataclass storing material parameters for an ionomer phase.

These parameters are consumed by the model classes in marapendi.models.membrane (e.g. IonomerModel, MembraneModel, PFSAModel) to compute derived quantities such as charge conductivity, water diffusivity, and sorption isotherms.

rho_dry_ion

Dry density of the ionomer [kg/m³].

Type:

float

EW_ion

Equivalent weight [kg/kmol].

Type:

float

darken_num_ion, darken_den_ion

Polynomial coefficients (numerator / denominator) for the Darken correction to water diffusivity.

Type:

np.ndarray

sorption_coeffs_ion

Polynomial coefficients for the equilibrium water-content isotherm.

Type:

np.ndarray

lmbd_liq_ref_ion

Reference liquid-equilibrium water content [mol H₂O / mol SO₃⁻].

Type:

float

D_lmbd_ref_ion

Reference water diffusivity [m²/s].

Type:

float

k_des_ref_ion

Reference desorption rate constant [m/s].

Type:

float

sigma_ref_ion

Reference ionic conductivity [S/m].

Type:

float

T_ref_sigma_ion, T_ref_D_ion, T_ref_des_ion

Reference temperatures for conductivity, diffusivity, and desorption Arrhenius corrections [K].

Type:

float

E_act_ion

Activation energy for ionic conductivity [J/kmol].

Type:

float

f_v_perc_ion

Percolation threshold for conductivity (volume fraction) [-].

Type:

float

n_sigma_ion

Exponent in the percolation conductivity model.

Type:

float

c_ion

Molar concentration of ionomer [kmol/m³] — computed in __post_init__ from rho_dry_ion / EW_ion.

Type:

float

V_ion

Molar volume of dry ionomer [m³/kmol] — computed in __post_init__.

Type:

float

charge_ion

Charge carrier type ('proton' or 'hydroxide').

Type:

str

D_lmbd_ref_ion: float = None
EW_ion: float = None
E_act_ion: float = None
T_ref_D_ion: float = None
T_ref_des_ion: float = None
T_ref_sigma_ion: float = None
V_ion: float = None
__post_init__()[source]

Compute c_ion and V_ion from density and equivalent weight. Skipped when either field has not been supplied yet.

c_ion: float = None
charge_ion: str = 'proton'
darken_den_ion: ndarray = None
darken_num_ion: ndarray = None
f_v_perc_ion: float = None
k_des_ref_ion: float = None
lmbd_liq_ref_ion: float = None
n_sigma_ion: float = None
rho_dry_ion: float = None
sigma_ref_ion: float = 1e-12
sorption_coeffs_ion: ndarray = None
class marapendi.components.ionomer.PFSAIonomer(rho_dry_ion: float = None, EW_ion: float = None, darken_num_ion: ndarray = None, darken_den_ion: ndarray = None, sorption_coeffs_ion: ndarray = None, lmbd_liq_ref_ion: float = None, D_lmbd_ref_ion: float = None, k_des_ref_ion: float = None, sigma_ref_ion: float = 1e-12, T_ref_sigma_ion: float = None, T_ref_D_ion: float = None, T_ref_des_ion: float = None, E_act_ion: float = None, f_v_perc_ion: float = None, n_sigma_ion: float = None, c_ion: float = None, V_ion: float = None, charge_ion: str = 'proton')[source]

Bases: Ionomer

Parameter dataclass for perfluorosulfonic acid ionomers (e.g. Nafion).

Inherits all fields from Ionomer. The corresponding computation methods (o2_permeability, h2_permeability, calculate_electroosmotic_drag_coefficient) live in marapendi.models.membrane.PFSAModel.

calculate_electroosmotic_drag_coefficient(T, lmbd)[source]
h2_permeability(T: float, f_v: float) → float[source]
o2_permeability(f_v, T=353.15)[source]
class marapendi.components.layer.Layer(name: str = 'layer', thickness: float = 0.0001, bulk_density: float = 2000.0, bulk_electrical_conductivity: float = 10000.0, bulk_specific_heat_capacity: float = 1000.0, bulk_thermal_conductivity: float = 1.0)[source]

Bases: Updatable

A class representing one layer of an AEM/PEM cell.

Identity-based equality: layers are unique physical instances in the cell model. Using value equality would cause ambiguous truth-value errors from numpy array fields and would incorrectly equate structurally identical but physically distinct layers (e.g. anode CL vs cathode CL).

bulk_density: float = 2000.0
bulk_electrical_conductivity: float = 10000.0
bulk_specific_heat_capacity: float = 1000.0
bulk_thermal_conductivity: float = 1.0
name: str = 'layer'
thickness: float = 0.0001

Module providing classes to model flow channels and their operating conditions in a PEM fuel cell. This includes representations for channel dimensions, flow rates, stoichiometries, and methods to compute gas transport resistances.

Classes

ChannelConditions

Encapsulates the conditions (temperature, RH, pressure, gas composition) for a flow channel.

FlowChannel

Represents a flow channel geometry and state in a PEM fuel cell, providing methods to calculate flow rates and gas transport resistances.

class marapendi.components.flow_channels.ChannelConditions(temperature: float, rh: float, pressure: float, dry_o2_mole_fraction: float, dry_h2_mole_fraction: float, stoichiometry: float)[source]

Bases: object

Class to encapsulate the operating conditions of the gas in a flow channels.

temperature

Temperature in Kelvin.

Type:

float

rh

Relative humidity (0-1).

Type:

float

pressure

Total pressure in Pa.

Type:

float

dry_o2_mole_fraction

Mole fraction of O2 in the dry gas mixture.

Type:

float

dry_h2_mole_fraction

Mole fraction of H2 in the dry gas mixture.

Type:

float

stoichiometry

Stoichiometric ratio (actual flow / required flow).

Type:

float

set_conditions(...)[source]

Update one or more of the channel operating conditions.

dry_h2_mole_fraction: float
dry_o2_mole_fraction: float
pressure: float
rh: float
set_conditions(temperature=None, rh=None, pressure=None, dry_o2_mole_fraction=None, dry_h2_mole_fraction=None, stoichiometry=None)[source]

Update the channel conditions.

Parameters:

  • temperature (float, optional) – New temperature in K.

  • rh (float, optional) – New relative humidity (0-1).

  • pressure (float, optional) – New total pressure in Pa.

  • dry_o2_mole_fraction (float, optional) – New dry O2 mole fraction.

  • dry_h2_mole_fraction (float, optional) – New dry H2 mole fraction.

  • stoichiometry (float, optional) – New stoichiometry value.

Notes

Only the provided arguments will update; others remain unchanged.

stoichiometry: float
temperature: float
class marapendi.components.flow_channels.FlowChannel(name: str = 'porous layer', thickness: float = 0.0001, bulk_density: float = 2000.0, bulk_electrical_conductivity: float = 10000.0, bulk_specific_heat_capacity: float = 1000.0, bulk_thermal_conductivity: float = 1.0, eps_p: float = 1, tort: float = 1.0, d_p: float = 2e-05, K_abs: float = 1e-11, n_rel: float = 3, n_s: float = 3, theta_contact: float = 120.0, eps_ion: float = 0.0, tau_ion: float = 1.0, p_b: float = 15020, van_genuchten_m: float = 0.7262, van_genuchten_n: float = 3.652, s_im: float = 0, reactant: str = 'o2', inlet_stoichiometry: float = 0, inlet_gas_flow_rate: float = <function FlowChannel.inlet_gas_flow_rate>, inlet_liquid_flow_rate: float = 0, inlet_liquid_saturation: float = 0, width: float = 0.001, height: float = 0.001, length: float = 0.1, channel_land_ratio: float = 1.0, n_parallel: int = 14, sherwood: float = 4.0, transport_resistance_model: ~marapendi.models.transport.ChannelGasResistanceModel = <factory>)[source]

Bases: PorousLayer

Class to represent a fuel cell flow channels, inheriting from PorousLayer to allow similar interface for transport calculations. Corresponds to channels of a single cell.

reactant

The primary reactant species (‘o2’ or ‘h2’).

Type:

str

inlet_stoichiometry

Inlet stoichiometric ratio.

Type:

float

inlet_gas_flow_rate[source]

Gas flow rate entering the channel (m3/s).

Type:

float

inlet_liquid_flow_rate

Liquid flow rate entering the channel (m3/s).

Type:

float

inlet_liquid_saturation

Saturation fraction of the inlet stream.

Type:

float

width

Channel width (m).

Type:

float

height

Channel height (m).

Type:

float

length

Channel length (m).

Type:

float

channel_land_ratio

Ratio of channel width to land width.

Type:

float

n_parallel

Number of parallel channels.

Type:

int

transport_resistance_model

Model to calculate total gas transport resistance.

Type:

ChannelGasResistanceModel

Derived Attributes
------------------
hydraulic_diameter

Hydraulic diameter of the channel (m).

Type:

float

channel_flow_section

Cross-sectional area of a single channel (m2).

Type:

float

total_flow_section

Total flow area accounting for all channels (m2).

Type:

float

RT

Product of universal gas constant and temperature (J/kmolK).

Type:

float

set_inlet_stoichiometry(stoichiometry)[source]

Set the inlet stoichiometry.

reactant_mole_fraction()

Return the mole fraction of the primary reactant.

set_fixed_inlet_gas_flow_rate(inlet_gas_flow_rate)[source]

Fix the inlet gas flow rate and update liquid saturation.

set_fixed_inlet_liquid_flow_rate(inlet_liquid_flow_rate)[source]

Fix the inlet liquid flow rate and update liquid saturation.

set_inlet_gas_flow_rate_from_stoichiometry(reactant_consumption, stoichiometry, fixed_inlet_gas_flow_rate)[source]

Compute inlet gas flow from stoichiometry and consumption.

calculate_inlet_gas_flow_rate(reactant_consumption)

Calculate gas flow rate based on stoichiometry and reactant consumption.

calculate_inlet_stochiometry(reactant_consumption)[source]

Compute effective stoichiometry from flow rate and consumption.

gas_transport_resistance(species, volume_flow_rate=None)[source]

Compute gas transport resistance in the channel.

__post_init__()[source]

Initialize geometric and thermodynamic properties after instantiation.

calculate_inlet_stochiometry(reactant_consumption, reactant_mole_fraction, gas_concentration)[source]

Compute stoichiometry from known flow rate and consumption.

Parameters:

reactant_consumption (float) – Reactant consumption rate (kmol/s).

Returns:

Effective stoichiometric ratio.

Return type:

float

channel_land_ratio: float = 1.0
eps_p: float = 1
gas_superficial_speed(volumetric_flow_rate: float = None) → float[source]

Calculate the gas superficial speed in the channel.

Parameters:

volumetric_flow_rate (float, optional) – Volumetric flow rate of the gas (m³/s). If None, uses the inlet gas flow rate.

Returns:

Gas superficial speed in the channel (m/s).

Return type:

float

gas_transport_resistance(species, volume_flow_rate=None)[source]

Calculate total gas transport resistance for a species.

Parameters:

  • species (str) – Species name (e.g., ‘o2’, ‘h2o’).

  • volume_flow_rate (float, optional) – Specific volume flow rate (m3/s), overrides default inlet gas flow.

Returns:

Total transport resistance (s/m).

Return type:

float

height: float = 0.001
inlet_gas_flow_rate(reactant_consumption, reactant_mole_fraction, gas_concentration)[source]

Calculate gas flow rate required by stoichiometry.

Parameters:

reactant_consumption (float) – Reactant consumption rate (kmol/s).

Returns:

Required inlet gas flow rate (m3/s).

Return type:

float

inlet_liquid_flow_rate: float = 0
inlet_liquid_saturation: float = 0
inlet_stoichiometry: float = 0
length: float = 0.1
liquid_to_gas_velocity_ratio(liquid_saturation, gas_viscosity, liquid_viscosity)[source]

Calculate the liquid-to-gas velocity ratio in the channel. Uses the eq. 10 of Zhang et al. (2026).

Note: Supposes kinematic viscosity of water for the inlet liquid.

Returns:

  • float – Ratio between liquid and gas velocity in the channel (n.d.).

  • Reference

  • ———

  • Zhang et al. Advancing Next-Generation Proton Exchange Membrane Fuel Cell Design through Multi-Physics and AI Modeling.

  • Energy Environ. Sci. 2026. https (//doi.org/10.1039/d5ee04599a.)

n_parallel: int = 14
outlet_liquid_flow_rate()[source]
outlet_liquid_molar_flow_rate()[source]
outlet_liquid_speed()[source]
reactant: str = 'o2'
set_fixed_inlet_gas_flow_rate(inlet_gas_flow_rate)[source]

Fix the inlet gas flow rate and update liquid saturation.

Parameters:

inlet_gas_flow_rate (float) – New gas flow rate (mol/s).

set_fixed_inlet_liquid_flow_rate(inlet_liquid_flow_rate)[source]

Fix the inlet liquid flow rate and update liquid saturation.

Parameters:

inlet_liquid_flow_rate (float) – New liquid flow rate (mol/s).

set_inlet_gas_flow_rate_from_stoichiometry(reactant_consumption, stoichiometry=0, fixed_inlet_gas_flow_rate=0)[source]

Set the inlet gas flow rate from stoichiometry and reactant consumption.

Parameters:

  • reactant_consumption (float) – Consumption rate of the reactant (kmol/s).

  • stoichiometry (float, optional) – Stoichiometric ratio to override (default 0, uses existing).

  • fixed_inlet_gas_flow_rate (float, optional) – Additional fixed flow rate to add (default 0).

set_inlet_stoichiometry(stoichiometry)[source]

Set the inlet stoichiometric ratio.

Parameters:

stoichiometry (float) – New stoichiometric value.

sherwood: float = 4.0
tort: float = 1.0
transport_resistance_model: ChannelGasResistanceModel
width: float = 0.001
class marapendi.components.electrolyte.ElectrolyteSolution(temperature: float = 298.15, molality: float = 0, weight_percent: float = 0, electrolyte_molecular_weight: float = 56.105)[source]

Bases: object

Class to represent an electrolyte solution, calculating various thermophysical and transport properties based on temperature and concentration.

temperature

Temperature of the solution [K].

Type:

float

molality

Molality of the solution [kmol/kg].

Type:

float

weight_percent

Weight percent concentration [0-1].

Type:

float

electrolyte_molecular_weight

Molecular weight of the electrolyte [kg/kmol].

Type:

float

__post_init__()[source]

Initialize and compute dependent properties.

calculate_density()[source]

Return a default water-like density, override in subclasses.

calculate_ionic_conductivity(temperature=None)[source]

Return a default very low ionic conductivity, override in subclasses.

calculate_molality()[source]

Calculate molality from weight percent.

Returns:

Molality [kmol/kg].

Return type:

float

calculate_molarity()[source]

Calculate molarity of the solution.

Returns:

Molarity [kmol/m³].

Return type:

float

calculate_saturation_pressure()[source]

Return water saturation pressure by default, override in subclasses.

calculate_surface_tension()[source]

Return water surface tension by default, override in subclasses.

calculate_weight_percent()[source]

Calculate weight percent from molality [kmol/kg].

Returns:

Weight percent [0-100].

Return type:

float

electrolyte_molecular_weight: float = 56.105
molality: float = 0
set_temperature(temperature)[source]

Set temperature and update dependent properties.

Parameters:

temperature (float) – New temperature [K].

temperature: float = 298.15
weight_percent: float = 0
class marapendi.components.electrolyte.KOH_solution(temperature: float = 298.15, molality: float = 0, weight_percent: float = 0, electrolyte_molecular_weight: float = 56.105)[source]

Bases: ElectrolyteSolution

Specialized class for aqueous KOH solutions, with empirical correlations for density, ionic conductivity and vapor pressure lowering.

calculate_density(temperature=300)[source]

Calculate solution density using correlation from Hodges et al. (2023).

Parameters:

  • weight_percent (float) – Weight percent [0-100].

  • temperature (float) – Temperature [K].

Returns:

  • float – Density [kg/m³].

  • Reference

  • ———

  • Hodges, A. et al. J. Chem. Eng. Data 68, 1485–1506 (2023).

calculate_ionic_conductivity(temperature=300)[source]

Calculate ionic conductivity using correlations from Hodges et al. (2023).

Returns:

  • float – Ionic conductivity [S/m].

  • Reference

  • ———

  • Hodges, A. et al. J. Chem. Eng. Data 68, 1485–1506 (2023).

calculate_saturation_pressure()[source]

Calculate vapor pressure lowering using correlation from Balej (1985). Used to compute the water activity of the electrolyte solution.

Parameters:

  • molality (float) – Molality [kmol/kg].

  • water_sat_pressure (float) – Pure water saturation pressure [Pa].

Returns:

  • float – Solution saturation pressure [Pa].

  • Reference

  • ———

  • Balej, J. International Journal of Hydrogen Energy 10, 233–243 (1985).

calculate_surface_tension() → float[source]

Calculate the surface tension of aqueous KOH using equation 10 in Hodges (2023).

Equation valid between 30 % wt. and 50 % wt.

Returns:

  • float – Solution surface tension in N/m

  • Reference

  • ———

  • Hodges, A. et al. J. Chem. Eng. Data 68, 1485–1506 (2023).
